title: Hamiltonian Paths vs. Circuits
description: A visual comparison of a Hamiltonian Path and a Hamiltonian Circuit on the same graph, highlighting the key difference of ending at the starting vertex.
elements: Graph G with 5 vertices (A, B, C, D, E)
Hamiltonian Path: A-B-C-D-E (visits each vertex once, ends on a different vertex)
Hamiltonian Circuit: A-B-C-D-E-A (visits each vertex once, ends on the starting vertex A)
relationships: The Hamiltonian Path is a sequence of edges that visits every vertex exactly once but does not form a cycle.
The Hamiltonian Circuit is a cycle that visits every vertex exactly once and returns to the start, making it a special type of Hamiltonian Path.
